No one can be bound by their own promise to do something that they are already forbidden from doing by God’s command. In other matters, the rule is that you should not break your word, even if you change your mind.

Two kinds of vows are considered: the vow of a daughter in her father's house. When her vow comes to her father's attention, he has the power to either confirm it or cancel it. The law is clear regarding a wife. If her husband allows her vow, even by remaining silent, it stands. If he disallows it, her obligation to her husband takes precedence; for she owes him submission as she does to the Lord. God’s law promotes good order in families. It is fitting that every man should rule in his own house and have his wife and children under his authority, rather than this important principle be broken or any encouragement be given to those of lesser status to break apart these bonds. God releases the obligation even of a solemn vow. Religion secures the well-being of all societies; and families on earth are blessed by it.
